channel.title: ThreatFeed — Security Advisory Aggregator
channel.description: Curated feed of actively-exploited vulnerabilities, zero-days, supply chain compromises, and ongoing attack campaigns. Advisory/CVE-focused. Refreshed every 6 hours.

item.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Leads June Activity — 9 New Victims
item.dc:creator: PurpleOps
item.description: High · The Gentlemen ransomware group posted 9 new victims, heavily impacting healthcare and professional-services sectors globally. Source: PurpleOps.

item.title: World Leaks Posts 6 New Victims in 24 Hours
item.dc:creator: PurpleOps
item.description: High · The World Leaks data-extortion group led recent activity with 6 new victims disclosed within 24 hours, alongside active groups PEAR and Akira. Source: PurpleOps.

item.title: PTC Windchill & FlexPLM Improper Input Validation Added to CISA KEV (CVE-2026-12569)
item.dc:creator: CISA
item.description: High · CVE-2026-12569 · CVSS 8.6 · Improper input validation in PTC Windchill and FlexPLM. Added to the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 25, 2026 based on evidence of active exploitation.

item.title: Cisco Unified Communications Manager SSRF Added to CISA KEV (CVE-2026-20230)
item.dc:creator: CISA
item.description: High · CVE-2026-20230 · CVSS 8.2 · Server-Side Request Forgery in Cisco Unified Communications Manager. Added to the CISA KEV catalog on June 25, 2026 on evidence of active exploitation.

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Affiliate Exploits Check Point VPN Flaw (CVE-2026-50751)
item.dc:creator: CYFIRMA
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-50751 · CVSS 9.1 · A Qilin ransomware affiliate is exploiting CVE-2026-50751 in Check Point VPNs for initial access, with a focus on network-infrastructure and supply chain vulnerabilities.

item.title: Unpatched SharePoint Servers Actively Exploited for Ransomware Deployment
item.dc:creator: Threat-Modeling.com
item.description: High · Unpatched on-prem SharePoint servers are being actively exploited as an initial-access vector for ransomware deployment. Patch and hunt for post-exploitation web shells.

item.title: Windows Netlogon RCE (CVE-2026-41089) Under Active Exploitation
item.dc:creator: Threat-Modeling.com
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-41089 · CVSS 9.8 · A critical Windows Netlogon remote code execution vulnerability now under active exploitation in the wild. Domain controllers are the primary target — patch immediately.

item.title: Malicious easy-day-js Package Injected into @mastra/* npm Org
item.dc:creator: GitGuardian
item.description: High · A coordinated supply chain attack pushed a malicious easy-day-js package into the dependency tree of the entire @mastra/* npm organization on June 17, 2026.

item.title: "RoguePlanet" Microsoft Defender Zero-Day — Patch In Progress
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer
item.description: High · Microsoft confirmed it is developing an out-of-band patch for the "RoguePlanet" Defender zero-day following reports of exploitation. Mitigations recommended until the fix ships.

item.title: Oracle PeopleSoft PeopleTools Zero-Day Exploited by ShinyHunters (CVE-2026-35273)
item.dc:creator: Rapid7
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-35273 · CVSS 9.8 · Unauthenticated SSRF-to-RCE in PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools 8.61/8.62. Mandiant confirmed zero-day exploitation by ShinyHunters (UNC6240) against higher education between May 27 and June 9, 2026. Oracle published an out-of-band alert on June 10.

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager — 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026 (CVE-2026-20245)
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-20245 · CVSS 9.8 · Improper output encoding in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager allowing arbitrary command execution as root — the seventh Cisco SD-WAN zero-day exploited in 2026. Added to CISA KEV on June 9.

item.title: Windows Kernel Wormable RCE (CVE-2026-45657, CVSS 9.8)
item.dc:creator: Zero Day Initiative
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-45657 · CVSS 9.8 · Windows Kernel RCE allowing remote, unauthenticated code execution at SYSTEM with no user interaction. Rated wormable — prioritize patching. Fixed in June 2026 Patch Tuesday.

item.title: Windows HTTP.sys Unauthenticated RCE (CVE-2026-47291, CVSS 9.8)
item.dc:creator: Zero Day Initiative
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-47291 · CVSS 9.8 · Remote, unauthenticated code execution in HTTP.sys with no user interaction. Any internet-facing Windows host running the HTTP protocol stack is at risk. Fixed in June 2026 Patch Tuesday.

item.title: Windows DHCP Client Service RCE (CVE-2026-44815, CVSS 9.8)
item.dc:creator: Zero Day Initiative
item.description: Critical · CVE-2026-44815 · CVSS 9.8 · Remote code execution in the Windows DHCP Client Service. Fixed in the record June 2026 Patch Tuesday release.

item.title: Microsoft Defender Elevation-of-Privilege Zero-Day Actively Exploited (CVE-2026-41091)
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
item.description: High · CVE-2026-41091 · CVSS 7.8 · Elevation-of-privilege in Microsoft Defender, exploited in the wild and patched in the record-breaking June 2026 Patch Tuesday (206 CVEs). Flagged as the most urgent zero-day of the month.

item.title: Arista EOS Incomplete Comparison Vulnerability Added to CISA KEV (CVE-2026-7473)
item.dc:creator: CISA
item.description: High · CVE-2026-7473 · CVSS 7.5 · Incomplete comparison with missing factors in Arista Extensible Operating System (EOS). Added to the CISA KEV catalog on June 9, 2026.

item.title: Google Chrome V8 Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild (CVE-2026-11645)
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
item.description: High · CVE-2026-11645 · CVSS 8.8 · Out-of-bounds read/write in the V8 JavaScript engine with an exploit in the wild. Google shipped an emergency Chrome update on June 8, 2026. CISA added it to KEV on June 9.

item.title: Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer Deserialization Added to CISA KEV (CVE-2026-45247)
item.dc:creator: CISA
item.description: High · CVE-2026-45247 · CVSS 8.1 · Deserialization of untrusted data in the Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer (Magento/Adobe Commerce extension). Added to the CISA KEV catalog on June 3, 2026.

item.title: Miasma Crosses Ecosystems: 73 Azure Repos Disabled, 37 Malicious PyPI Wheels
item.dc:creator: Phoenix Security
item.description: Critical · GitHub enforcement disabled 73 Microsoft Azure repositories in 105 seconds after AI coding-agent hooks were planted in Azure/durabletask. The worm then dropped 37 malicious PyPI wheels across 19 packages using .pth startup hooks that steal credentials on every Python invocation. Actor: TeamPCP (UNC6780).

item.title: Miasma: Self-Spreading npm Worm Hooks 13 AI Coding Tools (TeamPCP / UNC6780)
item.dc:creator: GitGuardian
item.description: Critical · A self-spreading worm injecting into the SessionStart hooks of 13 AI coding tools (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI), forging SLSA provenance to pass npm audit, using GitHub as a C2 channel, and shipping a DEADMAN_SWITCH that wipes developer machines if tokens are revoked. Compromised 32 packages under the @redhat-cloud-services npm namespace on June 1.

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud: 170+ npm & PyPI Packages Backdoored (CVE-2026-45321)
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek
item.description: High · CVE-2026-45321 · CVSS 8.6 · A new wave of Shai-Hulud supply chain attacks hit 170+ npm and PyPI packages, harvesting developer and CI/CD credentials.
